title: seldinger-lumen#53: Implement batched coaxial guidewire + catheter assemblies
overlap_handling: Existing open PR #77 already addressed issue #53; used and verified the existing overlapping branch instead of opening a duplicate PR.
implementation_summary: Batched coaxial construction builds one guidewire rod and one catheter rod per env in a single Newton model.
Per-env guidewire and catheter base actuation arrays are allocated and exercised by tests.
n_per_env_contact/n_cath_per_env map guidewire+catheter body blocks to the correct env for wall contact and coaxial coupling.
Coaxial coupling keeps each guidewire scoped to its env catheter centerline while preserving two-way reactions.
Solver support documentation marks coaxial batching closed and links issue #53 closure evidence.

local_checks.command: python -m pytest tests/test_newton_coaxial.py tests/test_newton_coaxial_coupling.py tests/test_newton_batched.py tests/test_solver_support_docs.py -q

mandatory_review.notes: Static scan found no added secret/injection/eval/pickle/SQL-formatting risks; git diff --check passed; targeted regression suite passed: 23 passed in 8.36s.

robinreview_state.issue_comment: Review done. Nothing worth flagging — looks clean to me.
robinreview_state.review_comment: No issues found; implementation correctly partitions catheter bodies per environment and passes necessary parameters to the coupling kernel.
robinreview_state.rerun_reason: No new code changes were pushed in this task run; existing Robin review is green on current head SHA.
